# Agent Design Spec: Technician Appointment Scheduling

*Generated by Agent Design Toolkit · Use case: Telecom field service scheduling*

---

## 1. Requirement Interpretation

**Agent goal:** Schedule technician appointments autonomously — diagnose whether a technician visit is required, check availability, confirm appointment, and send reminders — without agent transfer.

**Scope:**
- Service troubleshooting that cannot be resolved remotely — equipment failure, installation, line issues
- Appointment booking, rescheduling, and cancellation
- Proactive reminders and day-of confirmation

**Out of scope:**
- Commercial/business account scheduling (separate SLA windows and technician pools)
- Emergency same-day appointments (requires human dispatcher)
- Multi-unit building access coordination (requires property management contact)

**Step-by-step flow:**
1. Run remote diagnostics — confirm technician visit is necessary before scheduling
2. Retrieve available appointment windows for customer's service area
3. Present options — date, time window, technician arrival estimate
4. Confirm appointment with customer
5. Send confirmation via customer's preferred channel (SMS, email, app)
6. Schedule proactive reminder 24 hours and 2 hours before appointment

---

## 2. Open Decisions

| # | Decision | Options | Blocking? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Remote diagnostic required before scheduling? | Always / Only for service issues / Never | Yes |
| 2 | How many appointment windows to present? | 2 options / 3 options / Full availability calendar | Yes |
| 3 | Rescheduling cutoff — how close to appointment can customer reschedule? | 2 hours / 4 hours / 24 hours | Yes |
| 4 | What if no availability within 5 days? | Offer waitlist / Escalate to human / Show next available regardless | Yes |
| 5 | Confirmation channel — customer preference or system default? | Always ask / Use account preference / Default to SMS | No |

---

## 3. Knowledge Base Design

| Knowledge source | What it contains | Freshness required | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|
| Field dispatch system | Technician availability by service area and time window | Real-time | Engineering |
| Remote diagnostic API | Line health, signal quality, equipment status | Real-time | Engineering |
| Equipment inventory | CPE model, firmware version, known issues | Daily sync | Engineering |
| Service area polygons | Geographic routing to correct technician pool | Weekly sync | Operations |
| Appointment history | Prior visits, outcomes, recurring issues | On-demand | Engineering |

---

## 4. Memory Design

| Memory type | What to store | When to store | When to clear |
|---|---|---|---|
| Session memory | Diagnostic results, appointment options presented, confirmed slot | After each step | End of session |
| Cross-session memory | Prior appointment history, recurring issue flag | After scheduling | 12-month rolling window |
| Pre-session context | Open appointments, pending reschedules | Session initialization | End of session |

**MUST:** Check for existing open appointments before presenting new scheduling options — customer must not be double-booked
**MUST NOT:** Schedule appointment without completing remote diagnostic first

---

## 5. Tool Contracts

### `run_remote_diagnostic`
| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| customer_id | string | Yes | — |
| equipment_id | string | Yes | CPE identifier |
| diagnostic_type | enum | Yes | Full / Quick / Line-only |

**Returns:** Diagnostic result, recommended action (remote fix / technician required / equipment replacement)
**Latency SLA:** < 3 seconds — customer-facing, must not introduce perceptible delay
**Failure mode:** If unavailable → assume technician required, proceed to scheduling with flag

---

### `get_appointment_availability`
| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| service_area_id | string | Yes | — |
| date_range | object | Yes | start_date, end_date — default next 7 days |
| appointment_type | enum | Yes | Installation / Repair / Equipment swap |

**Returns:** Available windows with date, time range, estimated arrival window
**Failure mode:** If unavailable → inform customer of system delay, offer callback to confirm

---

### `book_appointment`
| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| customer_id | string | Yes | — |
| appointment_slot_id | string | Yes | From availability response |
| contact_number | string | Yes | For technician day-of contact |
| access_instructions | string | No | Gate codes, building access notes |

**Returns:** Confirmation number, technician arrival window, cancellation instructions
**Idempotent:** No — duplicate prevention enforced at dispatch system level
**Confidence threshold:** ≥ 0.90 — appointment booking is an irreversible action

---

## 6. Logging Schema

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| session_id | string | customer_id + care_episode |
| diagnostic_result | enum | Remote fix / Technician required / Equipment replacement |
| appointment_booked | boolean | — |
| appointment_slot_id | string | Null if not booked |
| days_to_appointment | integer | Lead time from booking to visit |
| confirmation_channel | enum | SMS / Email / App / None |
| reschedule_count | integer | Times rescheduled prior to visit |
| timestamp | datetime | UTC |

**Alert triggers:** Scheduling failure rate > 2% · Average days to appointment > 5 · Reschedule rate > 25%

---

## 7. Eval Design

### Happy path
| Case | Input | Expected output |
|---|---|---|
| Service outage requiring technician | "My internet has been down for 2 days" | Runs diagnostic, confirms technician required, presents 3 windows, books appointment, sends confirmation |
| Equipment replacement | "My modem keeps restarting" | Diagnoses equipment failure, schedules swap appointment with equipment note to dispatch |
| Rescheduling existing appointment | "Can I move my appointment to Friday?" | Retrieves existing booking, presents Friday availability, confirms reschedule |

### Edge cases
| Case | Input | Expected output |
|---|---|---|
| Issue resolvable remotely | "My internet is slow" | Runs diagnostic, resolves remotely — does not schedule unnecessary visit |
| No availability within 5 days | "I need someone to come out" | Presents next available beyond 5 days, offers waitlist, explains delay |
| Customer already has open appointment | "I need to schedule a technician" | Surfaces existing appointment first — asks if they want to keep or reschedule |

### Failure cases
| Case | Expected behavior |
|---|---|
| Dispatch system unavailable | Informs customer of scheduling system delay — offers callback within 2 hours to confirm slot |
| Remote diagnostic times out | Assumes technician required with flag — proceeds to scheduling, logs diagnostic miss |
| Booking fails after customer confirms | Holds slot for 10 minutes — retries once — escalates to human dispatcher if retry fails |

### Adversarial cases
| Case | Expected behavior |
|---|---|
| Customer requests same-day emergency | Explains same-day availability not guaranteed — escalates to human dispatcher |
| Customer provides incorrect access instructions | Confirms instructions back to customer before booking — does not proceed on ambiguous access info |
| Customer requests specific technician | Explains technician assignment is by availability — does not override dispatch routing |
